questions.question: Does deleting a workspace cancel your subscription automatically?

options.text: Yes, the subscription is canceled immediately upon deletion.
options.explanation: Deleting a workspace does not cancel a subscription. You must downgrade your plan before deleting.

options.text: No, you must downgrade your plan before deleting the workspace.
options.explanation: According to Notion Help, deleting a workspace does not cancel a subscription. You should downgrade first to stop billing.

options.text: Yes, but you will be refunded the remaining balance.
options.explanation: Refunds are not mentioned in the context of deleting a workspace. The focus is on downgrading the plan before deletion.

options.text: No, the subscription remains active for 30 days.
options.explanation: The subscription is not automatically paused or canceled; it remains active until you manually downgrade.

questions.question: What happens to private pages inside a workspace after deletion?

options.text: They are moved to a trash folder that can be restored within 30 days.
options.explanation: All content, including private pages, is permanently deleted and cannot be recovered.

options.text: They are permanently deleted and cannot be accessed.
options.explanation: The article states that all content inside a workspace will be deleted, and you won't be able to access your content — even private pages.

options.text: They are automatically backed up to a local directory.
options.explanation: There is no automatic backup mechanism mentioned; you must manually export data you wish to keep.

options.text: They become public and accessible to other users.
options.explanation: Private pages are not made public; they are simply deleted from existence.

questions.question: How do you access the option to delete an entire workspace?

options.text: Settings -> Team -> Danger zone -> Delete entire workspace
options.explanation: The correct path is Settings -> Workspace -> General -> Danger zone, not Team.

options.text: Settings -> Workspace -> General -> Danger zone
options.explanation: The step-by-step guide instructs you to go to Settings, select 'Workspace' under General, and scroll down to the Danger zone.

options.text: Sidebar -> Danger zone -> Delete entire workspace
options.explanation: The delete option is located within the Settings menu, not directly in the sidebar.

options.text: Account settings -> Workspace -> Delete entire workspace
options.explanation: The option is found under the 'Workspace' section of the Settings menu, not Account settings.

questions.question: Who loses access to the workspace if it is deleted?

options.text: Only the workspace owner.
options.explanation: Access is removed for 'everyone in it', meaning all users who were part of the workspace before deletion.

options.text: Only users who have not visited the workspace in the last 30 days.
options.explanation: All users who were part of the workspace are affected, regardless of their recent activity.

options.text: Everyone in it.
options.explanation: The article states the deleted workspace will be deleted for everyone in it, so no users retain access.

options.text: Only guests or external collaborators.
options.explanation: All members, including owners and editors, lose access.

questions.question: What is the final confirmation step required to delete a workspace?

options.text: Selecting 'I understand' from a dropdown menu.
options.explanation: You are asked to type the name of your workspace to confirm, not select a menu option.

options.text: Entering a confirmation code sent to your email.
options.explanation: There is no email confirmation code mentioned; the confirmation is typing the workspace name.

options.text: Typing the name of your workspace.
options.explanation: The guide explicitly states: 'You'll be asked to type the name of your workspace to confirm you want to proceed.'

options.text: Entering your password again.
options.explanation: A password is not required for this confirmation step; typing the workspace name is the method used.

questions.question: What happens to your account navigation immediately after deleting a workspace?

options.text: You are taken to the Notion Help Center.
options.explanation: You are returned to another workspace you belong to, or the sign-up page if you belong to no others.

options.text: You are returned to another workspace you belong to, or the sign-up page.
options.explanation: Once deleted, you are redirected to another workspace you manage or the sign-up page if you don't have access to any.

options.text: You are logged out and redirected to the login screen.
options.explanation: You are not logged out; you are redirected to an active workspace or sign-up page.

options.text: You are taken to the home page of the app.
options.explanation: You are not taken to the home page; you are returned to a workspace you belong to.

questions.question: Why should you export your data before deleting a workspace?

options.text: To ensure the data is backed up to the cloud.
options.explanation: Notion does not back up data upon deletion; you must perform the export manually to preserve your data.

options.text: To keep a copy of any data you want to keep, as all content will be deleted.
options.explanation: The article warns that all content will be deleted and advises exporting data you wish to keep before proceeding.

options.text: To make the deletion process faster.
options.explanation: Exporting data does not speed up the deletion process; it is a separate step for data preservation.

options.text: To send a notification to other workspace members.
options.explanation: Exporting is for data preservation, not for notifying other users.

questions.question: Is the action of deleting a workspace reversible?

options.text: Yes, if you export the data before deleting.
options.explanation: The action is permanent; even if you exported data, the workspace itself is deleted and cannot be undone.

options.text: No, this action is permanent.
options.explanation: The article explicitly states that 'This action is permanent' and that you won't be able to access your content.

options.text: Yes, but only if you contact Notion support within 24 hours.
options.explanation: There is no mention of support intervention restoring a deleted workspace.

options.text: No, unless you have a paid recovery plan.
options.explanation: No recovery plan is mentioned; the deletion is absolute.
